title: Reading stem and leaf plots
topic_path: Exploring one-variable quantitative data: Displaying and describing
Representing a quantitative variable with histograms and stem plots

assessment_items.question_content:
A zookeeper created the following stem-and-leaf plot showing the number of sloths at each major zoo in the country: 

  $ \left| \quad \begin{matrix} 0 \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ 1 \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ 2 \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ 3 \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ 4 \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \end{matrix} \quad \right| \quad \begin{matrix} 3& \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ 6&7&7& \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ 0&4& \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ 0&1&1&2&4&7&9&9& \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ 1&2&3&5&7& \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \end{matrix} $

Key: $4\,|\,1=41$ sloths

  ** How many zoos have exactly $17$ sloths? **

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] zoos
hints: The left column of the stem and leaf plot represents the tens place; each number on the right side represents the ones place for the number of sloths at a zoo.

We can combine the tens and ones places and write out the number of sloths at each zoo on the right: 

  $ \small{ \left| \space \begin{matrix} \color{\#28AE7B}{0} \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \color{\#28AE7B}{1} \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \color{\#28AE7B}{2} \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \color{\#28AE7B}{3} \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \color{\#28AE7B}{4} \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \end{matrix} \space \right| \space \begin{matrix} \color{\#6495ED}{3}& \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \color{\#6495ED}{6}&\color{\#6495ED}{7}&\color{\#6495ED}{7}& \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \color{\#6495ED}{0}&\color{\#6495ED}{4}& \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \color{\#6495ED}{0}&\color{\#6495ED}{1}&\color{\#6495ED}{1}&\color{\#6495ED}{2}&\color{\#6495ED}{4}&\color{\#6495ED}{7}&\color{\#6495ED}{9}&\color{\#6495ED}{9}& \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \color{\#6495ED}{1}&\color{\#6495ED}{2}&\color{\#6495ED}{3}&\color{\#6495ED}{5}&\color{\#6495ED}{7}& \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \end{matrix} \space \begin{matrix} \rightarrow \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \rightarrow \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \rightarrow \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \rightarrow \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \rightarrow \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \end{matrix} \space \begin{matrix} \color{\#28AE7B}{0}\color{\#6495ED}{3}& \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \color{\#28AE7B}{1}\color{\#6495ED}{6}&\color{\#28AE7B}{1}\color{\#6495ED}{7}&\color{\#28AE7B}{1}\color{\#6495ED}{7}& \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \color{\#28AE7B}{2}\color{\#6495ED}{0}&\color{\#28AE7B}{2}\color{\#6495ED}{4}& \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \color{\#28AE7B}{3}\color{\#6495ED}{0}&\color{\#28AE7B}{3}\color{\#6495ED}{1}&\color{\#28AE7B}{3}\color{\#6495ED}{1}&\color{\#28AE7B}{3}\color{\#6495ED}{2}&\color{\#28AE7B}{3}\color{\#6495ED}{4}&\color{\#28AE7B}{3}\color{\#6495ED}{7}&\color{\#28AE7B}{3}\color{\#6495ED}{9}&\color{\#28AE7B}{3}\color{\#6495ED}{9}& \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \color{\#28AE7B}{4}\color{\#6495ED}{1}&\color{\#28AE7B}{4}\color{\#6495ED}{2}&\color{\#28AE7B}{4}\color{\#6495ED}{3}&\color{\#28AE7B}{4}\color{\#6495ED}{5}&\color{\#28AE7B}{4}\color{\#6495ED}{7}& \vphantom{\Large{0}} \\ \end{matrix} } $
